summary.intent: Fix a UI bug where browser sessions incorrectly showed as 'paused' immediately after switching to another session.
summary.outcome: Fixed the badge logic in page-browser.js to use the server-side `sess.screencasting` field from the session list API instead of client-side state, so sessions with an active server-side screencast are correctly labeled.

repo: Session screencast state is tracked both client-side (screencasting.value) and server-side (sess.screencasting from the session list API); these can diverge.
The 'paused' badge was purely client-side: any session with a URL that wasn't the active screencasting session showed as paused, regardless of actual server state.
stop_screencast is no longer sent on session switch, meaning the screencast continues running on the server when switching sessions.

code.finding: Badge logic was: isActive && screencasting.value → 'live', else if has URL → 'paused'. Fixed to use sess.screencasting from API to determine actual server screencast state.

workflow: Lint and commit/push were run as sequential steps after the edit.

friction: Client-side and server-side screencast state were out of sync, making the bug non-obvious — the UI appeared correct in isolation but was wrong after a session switch.
